explicit_text: My parents fled their home after the Vietnam War ended because they were afraid of what might happen if they stayed. Many Hmongs, including my family, were persecuted and had to escape into the jungle. The war's aftermath forced thousands to become refugees, and my parents ended up in refugee camps in Thailand before coming to the United States.

explicit_text: After the Vietnam War, my parents became refugees and lived in refugee camps in Thailand. It was a difficult time for them, but it was a necessary step before they could come to the United States. Thailand was a temporary home where many Hmong families, including mine, stayed while seeking safety and a new beginning.

explicit_text: My parents came to the United States after being refugees in Thailand, hoping to build a better life for our family. We settled in Fresno, California, where they worked hard and eventually rented a bigger house to accommodate our large family. Although the language was difficult for them, they slowly learned English while raising many children, including me.
